Container.Name: obsidian-git-backup
Container.Overview:
Syncs an Obsidian vault via obsidian-headless (official Obsidian Sync client) and automatically backs it up to a git repository.
Every time Obsidian Sync delivers changes, they are committed and pushed to your git remote — giving you a private, versioned backup of your vault with full history.
Features: - Continuous sync via obsidian-headless - Filesystem-event-driven git commits (no polling) - Configurable debounce period for large vaults - Automatic SSH key generation on first run - Works with E2E encrypted vaults (decrypted locally before git commit)
This container has no web UI and exposes no ports. Monitor it via container logs.
Requires an Obsidian Sync subscription and a git repository (GitHub, GitLab, etc). If your vault uses E2E encryption, expand Advanced View to enter your E2EE password.
Run one container per vault. See the project README for full setup instructions.
Container.Requires: An Obsidian Sync subscription and a git repository with SSH access. Before installing: on your desktop or laptop (not the Unraid server), install Node.js, then run 'npm install -g obsidian-headless' and 'ob login' to obtain your auth token. A web browser is required for the login step.

Config.Name: Obsidian Auth Token
Config.Description: Your Obsidian account auth token. Treat this like a password — anyone with it can read ALL your synced vaults (not just this one). To get it: on your desktop/laptop, install obsidian-headless (npm install -g obsidian-headless), run 'ob login' (opens a browser), then copy the token from ~/.obsidian-headless/auth_token (macOS) or ~/.config/obsidian-headless/auth_token (Linux). See README for details.

Config.Name: Vault Name
Config.Description: The name of your Obsidian vault exactly as shown in the Obsidian app (e.g. 'My Notes'). Must match exactly — case-sensitive, including spaces. Run 'ob sync-list-remote' on your desktop to list available vaults.

Config.Name: Git Remote URL
Config.Description: SSH URL of the git remote to push to. Example: git@github.com:username/vault-backup.git. After first start, check container logs for the generated SSH public key and add it to your git server.

Config.Name: Config Storage
Config.Description: Stores SSH keys and obsidian-headless device state. Must survive restarts. If lost, you'll need to re-add the SSH public key to your git server and restart to re-register with Obsidian Sync.

Config.Name: Vault Storage
Config.Description: Vault data and git working tree. Strongly recommended — without this, the container re-clones the entire vault on every restart (slow, degrades git history). On Unraid, consider placing on your cache drive for better I/O with frequent small file changes.

Config.Name: Git Branch
Config.Description: Git branch to commit and push to. The branch must already exist on the remote.

Config.Name: Debounce Seconds
Config.Description: Seconds of filesystem quiet before committing. After detecting changes, the watcher waits this long for more changes before committing. For large vaults (10,000+ notes) or slow connections, consider 60-120.

Config.Name: E2EE Password
Config.Description: End-to-end encryption password. Only needed if the vault uses E2E encryption. Note: obsidian-headless decrypts vault contents locally — your git repository will contain plaintext files. E2EE protects the Obsidian Sync transport only.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for file permissions. Defaults to 99 (Unraid's 'nobody' user), which is correct for most Unraid setups. Change only if your appdata files need different ownership.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for file permissions. Defaults to 100 (Unraid's 'users' group). Change only if you have a specific group ownership requirement.
